_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-2 Abdominal Pain (Non-Traumatic) (Revised 05/03/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-2: Abdominal Pain (Non-Traumatic) — MDHHS Approved 5/3/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: Abdominal Pain (Non-Traumatic)
points: Conduct physical exam of abdomen including assessment of central and bilateral distal pulses
If symptoms of shock present, refer to Shock-Treatment Protocol
Position patient in a position of comfort if pain is non-traumatic; if trauma related, refer to General Trauma-Treatment Protocol
Do not allow patient to drink or eat anything (does not include ODT medications)
If patient is experiencing nausea and vomiting, refer to Nausea and Vomiting-Treatment Protocol
Treat pain per Pain Management-Procedure Protocol
Consider 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection — may be a BLS or Specialist procedure) per 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol

mca_selections_required.description: May be designated as a BLS skill or Specialist (AEMT/Paramedic) skill

deterioration_flags: Signs of shock (hypotension, tachycardia, diaphoresis, altered mental status) — refer to Shock-Treatment Protocol immediately
Absent or asymmetric distal pulses — consider vascular emergency (AAA); prompt transport, ALS response
scope_notes: 12-Lead ECG: scope per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
Pain management: refer to Pain Management-Procedure Protocol for scope by level
IV/IO access and fluids: Specialist skill unless MCA-approved otherwise
